title: Community Page: Full Layout with Tabs, Posts, and Customization

requirements.summary: Wire CommunityCard 'Open' button to navigate to CommunityPage within Communities tab
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Open' on a community card renders the CommunityPage for that community
Navigating away from the Communities tab and returning still allows re-opening the community page
The CommunityPage is rendered within the Communities tab without affecting any other tab or route

file_operations.description: Add state to track the active community ID. When a community is selected, store its ID in component state. Render CommunityPage component when an active community is set, passing the community ID as a prop. Provide a back/close handler to return to the hub list view.
Accept an onOpen callback prop. Wire the 'Open' button onClick to call onOpen with the community ID, allowing the parent (CommunitiesHubPage) to handle navigation to the CommunityPage.

requirements.summary: Create CommunityPage component with banner, stats bar, and horizontal tab bar
acceptanceCriteria: Banner image fills the full width of the community page header
If no banner image is set, the banner renders with the community's bannerColor as background
Stats bar displays current member count and post count fetched from the backend
Tab bar renders all six tabs: Home, Chat, Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules
Only the authenticated host sees the 'Edit Banner' control

file_operations.description: Create a new CommunityPage component that accepts a communityId prop. Fetch community data (name, bannerBlob, bannerColor, bannerFont, accentColor) from backend. Render a full-width banner section at the top displaying the banner image (if set) or a solid color fallback using bannerColor. Display the community name overlaid on the banner. Include a host-only 'Edit Banner' button rendered conditionally based on isCommunityHost backend check. Below the banner, render a stats bar showing member count and post count (fetch via getPosts and count posts, placeholder for member count). Below the stats bar, render a horizontal tab bar with six tabs: Home, Chat, Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules. Use local state to track the active tab and render the corresponding tab content below the tab bar.

Create a hook that fetches community data including banner settings (bannerBlob, bannerColor, bannerFont, accentColor), tab order via getTabOrder, and tab permissions via getTabPermissions. Return community metadata, loading state, and error state. Use React Query with a queryKey based on communityId for caching and automatic refetching.

Create a hook that fetches posts for a community using the getPosts backend method. Accept communityId as a parameter. Return posts array, loading state, error state, and a refetch function. Use React Query with polling (5-second interval) to keep posts up to date.

requirements.summary: Implement host-only banner customization with inline editor/modal
acceptanceCriteria: Host can upload a banner image (PNG/JPG, max 5MB); it is stored on-chain and displayed immediately
Host can choose a background color used when no image is uploaded
Host can select a font for the community title from at least 4 preset options
Host can select an accent color applied to buttons and highlights in the community page
Non-host members do not see the 'Edit Banner' control
Changes persist across page reloads

file_operations.description: Create a dialog component for banner customization. Include an image upload input for the banner image (validate PNG/JPG, max 5MB). Include color pickers for bannerColor and accentColor. Include a font selector dropdown with at least 4 preset font options (e.g., Sans-serif, Serif, Monospace, Display). On save, call updateBannerSettings backend method with the selected values (use ExternalBlob for banner image upload if provided, convert to Uint8Array). Display upload progress and handle errors. On success, invalidate community data queries to refresh the banner immediately.

Import and render BannerCustomizationDialog component. Wire the 'Edit Banner' button to open the dialog. Pass current banner settings as initial values to the dialog. The dialog should only be accessible to the community host (check via isCommunityHost backend method).

Create a mutation hook for updating banner settings. Accept communityId and banner settings (bannerBlob, bannerColor, bannerFont, accentColor) as parameters. Call updateBannerSettings backend method. On success, invalidate the community data query to refresh the banner. Return mutation state (loading, error, success).

requirements.summary: Implement draggable/reorderable tabs with per-member permissions
acceptanceCriteria: Tabs are draggable and reorderable for the host
Tab order is saved to the backend after each reorder and restored on next page load
Host can grant or revoke tab-reorder permission to individual members via a permissions UI within the community page
Members without permission see static, non-draggable tabs
Members with granted permission can also drag and reorder tabs; their changes persist on-chain

file_operations.description: Create a draggable tab bar component using a drag-and-drop library (e.g., dnd-kit or react-beautiful-dnd). Accept tabs array, activeTab, onTabChange, onReorder callbacks, and a canReorder boolean prop. If canReorder is true, enable drag-and-drop. On drop, call onReorder with the new tab order. Render each tab as a draggable item with visual feedback during drag.

Replace the static tab bar with the DraggableTabBar component. Fetch tab order from useCommunityData hook. Check if the current user can reorder tabs by calling isCommunityHostOrPermitted backend method. Pass canReorder boolean to DraggableTabBar. On reorder, call updateTabOrder backend method with the new order and invalidate the tab order query.

Create a dialog for managing tab reorder permissions (host-only). Display a list of community members with checkboxes indicating whether each member has tab-reorder permission. Fetch current permissions via getTabPermissions backend method. On toggle, call grantTabReorderPermission or revokeTabReorderPermission backend method. Display loading and error states. On success, invalidate the permissions query.

Add a host-only 'Manage Permissions' button in the CommunityPage header or settings area. Wire it to open the TabPermissionsDialog component. Only render this button if the current user is the community host.

Create hooks for tab management: useUpdateTabOrder mutation hook (calls updateTabOrder backend method), useGrantTabPermission and useRevokeTabPermission mutation hooks (call grantTabReorderPermission and revokeTabReorderPermission backend methods). Each hook should invalidate relevant queries on success.

requirements.summary: Implement Home tab with post creation form and chronological feed
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users cannot submit posts; a login prompt is shown instead
Authenticated users can type a post and optionally attach an image
Submitted posts appear in the feed immediately and persist on-chain
Post images are stored as binary on-chain and rendered inline in the feed
Each post displays the author's display name (or shortened principal), content, image (if any), and formatted timestamp

file_operations.description: Create the Home tab component. At the top, render a post creation form with a textarea for content and an optional image upload input. Show the form only to authenticated users; for unauthenticated users, display a 'Login to post' message with a link to the login flow. Below the form, render a feed of posts fetched via useCommunityPosts hook. For each post, display the author's name (fetch via useUserDisplayName hook or fallback to shortened principal), post content, optional image (render inline using data URL from imageBlob), and formatted timestamp (use time.ts utility). On form submit, validate input, call createPost backend method with content and imageBlob (convert image to Uint8Array), and invalidate the posts query to refresh the feed.

Create a mutation hook for creating posts. Accept communityId, content, and optional imageBlob as parameters. Convert imageBlob to Uint8Array if provided. Call createPost backend method. On success, invalidate the community posts query to refresh the feed. Return mutation state (loading, error, success).

Create a reusable PostItem component that accepts a CommunityPost object. Render the author's display name (fetch via useUserDisplayName), post content, optional image (decode imageBlob Uint8Array to data URL and render as img), and formatted timestamp (use formatTime utility from time.ts). Style the post card with proper spacing and borders.
Import and render HomeTab component when the Home tab is active. Pass communityId as a prop to HomeTab.

requirements.summary: Implement Chat tab as a non-functional greyed-out placeholder
acceptanceCriteria: Chat tab renders a visually recognizable greyed-out chat UI
No interactive elements in the Chat tab are functional
A 'Coming Soon' or equivalent label is prominently displayed
The mockup does not make any backend calls

file_operations.description: Create a placeholder component for the Chat tab. Render a greyed-out, non-interactive chat interface mockup with a message input bar (disabled), mock message bubbles (static, non-clickable), and a prominent 'Coming Soon' or 'Private messaging — coming soon' label overlay. Use opacity and disabled styles to visually indicate the tab is not functional.
Import and render ChatTabPlaceholder component when the Chat tab is active.

requirements.summary: Implement placeholder layouts for Lore, Polls, Quizzes, and Rules tabs
acceptanceCriteria: Lore, Polls, Quizzes, and Rules tabs each render a distinct placeholder layout
Each placeholder displays the tab name as a heading and a descriptive 'Coming Soon' message
Placeholder styling uses the community's active accent color and font settings

file_operations.description: Create a generic TabPlaceholder component that accepts tabName and accentColor as props. Render a styled placeholder card with the tab name as a heading and a 'Coming Soon' message. Apply the accentColor to borders or highlights. Use the community's font settings (if available from context or props).
Import and render TabPlaceholder component for Lore, Polls, Quizzes, and Rules tabs when they are active. Pass the tab name and the community's accentColor as props to each placeholder.

requirements.summary: Preserve active community ID when navigating away and returning to Communities tab
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to another tab and back to Communities preserves the open community page
If no community was open, the Communities hub list is shown as normal
No data or state outside the Communities tab is affected

file_operations.description: Use React state to track the active community ID. Persist this state across tab navigation by storing it in component state (not URL params or sessionStorage). When the user navigates away from the Communities tab and returns, the state is preserved by React's component lifecycle. If an active community ID is set, render CommunityPage; otherwise, render the hub list view.

requirements.summary: Ensure all changes are scoped to Communities tab and its sub-components
acceptanceCriteria: SessionPage, LobbyPage, chat components, document components, and all non-community files remain unchanged
No new routes or navigation entries outside the Communities tab are added

file_operations.description: Consolidate all community-related navigation and state management within CommunitiesHubPage. Do not add new routes or modify routing logic outside this file. Ensure CommunityPage is rendered inline within the Communities tab view.
